subUnits.title: 5.1 Meaning & Place Value of Decimals
notes: Decimal fractions are fractions whose denominators are powers of 10 (10, 100, 1000, etc.).
A decimal point (.) separates the whole number part from the fractional part.
Place values after the decimal: 1st digit = tenths (0.1), 2nd = hundredths (0.01), 3rd = thousandths (0.001).
Example: 4.35 = 4 + 3/10 + 5/100. The digit 3 is in the tenths place, 5 is in hundredths.

quiz.q: What does the decimal point separate?
quiz.explanation: The decimal point divides the whole number part (left) from the fractional part (right).

quiz.q: In 7.28, what is the place value of 8?
quiz.explanation: The second digit after the decimal is the hundredths place.

subUnits.title: 5.2 Relationship Between Fractions & Decimals
notes: Every fraction can be written as a decimal by dividing the numerator by the denominator.
To convert a decimal to a fraction, write the digits over 10, 100, or 1000 based on decimal places, then simplify.
Practice: Use money (₦0.50 = ½) and measurements to see the real-life connection.

quiz.q: Convert 0.6 to a fraction in lowest terms.
